name: Band Bent-Over Row
resistance_profile: Ascending band tension — resistance peaks at the top of the row as the band stretches, with little load at the bottom stretch.
cues: Hinge over and keep the back flat
Drive the elbows back and behind you, not out wide
Squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top
execution_steps: Stand on the middle of a resistance band with feet hip-width, holding a handle or the band in each hand.
Hinge at the hips to a flat-back, bent-over position with a slight knee bend and let the arms hang straight down.
Row both hands toward your lower ribs, driving the elbows back and behind the torso.
Squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top of the pull.
Lower under control until the arms are fully extended and the band tension eases, then repeat.
good_when: Home or travel training with no free weights available
A joint-friendly rowing option that stays easy on the lower back at light loads
Adding upper-back volume or a high-rep pump finisher
bad_when: You can progressively overload with free weights or a machine (bands cap the usable load)
You want maximal mid-back stimulus at the stretch (band tension is lowest there)
common_errors: Standing up and using momentum to swing the weight up
Rounding the lower back in the hinge
Pulling the hands to the belly instead of driving the elbows back
progressions: Use a heavier band or double the band up
Add a pause and a slow negative at the top
regressions: Perform it seated with the band looped around the feet for more support
